page: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, the instance features 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing two threads per core. The CPU allocation is dedicated, and the processor cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 55 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled GPUs or local storage.

Benchmark evaluations place the e2-standard-32 predominantly in the average performance tier. Multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Passmark, falls within the middle 50% of tested servers, as do memory bandwidth speeds for both cached and uncached operations. Single-core performance presents a mixed profile: stress-ng single-core testing ranks in the top 25%, whereas Geekbench single-core and text processing scores fall into the bottom 25%. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, demonstrate poor performance in the bottom 10%, though multi-threaded decompression recovers to average levels.

From a resource density perspective, the e2-standard-32 offers a balanced ratio of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring moderate, steady-state compute and memory without the premium cost of specialized hardware. It is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and small-to-medium language model inference, where extreme single-threaded speed is not a critical bottleneck.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor at 2.2 GHz. With 16 physical cores and hyper-threading, it provides dedicated CPU resources but lacks bundled GPUs and local storage. Performance benchmarks generally place this instance in the average tier, with multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations scoring in the middle 50% of tested systems. While single-core performance is mixed—showing strength in stress-ng but weakness in Geekbench and single-threaded compression—the instance maintains reliable multi-threaded capabilities. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient option for balanced workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 is a general-purpose instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced multi-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-32 features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel Xeon cores for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated vCPUs and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of memory for steady-state workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM on an x86_64 Intel Xeon architecture
Dedicated CPU allocation with 16 physical cores and hyper-threading
Average multi-core performance and memory bandwidth across standard benchmarks
No bundled GPU or local storage resources
Suitable for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
